reviews.review_msg: The class is very confusing, unorganized and prepare to teach yourself everything!

He makes this SCM class quite tough for no reason. He's a great guy, but not a very good professor; disorganized, often late, no feedback on projects, etc. Also, he presents only qualitative content while the test is heavily quantitative. Not terribly difficult, but no one knew how to calculate anything so the average was quite low.

Contents wise, it's straight forward. His quizzes and exams are not considered extremely hard; but going into final exam unprepared is difficult. Had we been given a few more practice scenarios, results could be significantly higher!

As an upcoming BBA graduate in Operations Management, I was really excited to steep myself into Supply Chain Management. Unfortunately, the instructor was not very engaging in class, content was purely theorical with very few calculation-based exercises from which the exam was 40% of final grade. I would avoid taking him in any class.

He failed to mention until a few days before our final (worth 40% of grade) that it would be made up of mainly calculation/long answer questions. He had never walked us through a quantitative problems, did not give us opportunities to practice calculations and all previous quizzes only tested on theory. Never replies to emails either

Prof teaches at UBC Sauder, not even kidding, the slides have boxes that cover up important text and other material. There is literally 0 consideration for the students when the materials are so disorganized. Not to mention that simple email replies take many days to receive, seriously? I don't know how nobody else has complained about this! Wow

Prof started teaching at UBC Sauder - Class is stale and difficult to follow along with. Quizzes are tough and extremely nuanced; lectures do not prepare you to do well and you are required to self-study the textbook. Class discussions dive deep into nuances not relevant for later examination. I would not recommend this course.

I'm sharing this feedback constructively. Prof. began teaching at UBC Sauder, but the course experience does not yet meet standards. A static lecture style makes it hard to stay engaged, group details are collected by email rather than Canvas, and a 45-minute presentation was assigned with one week's notice without sufficient class foundation.
The course was practical, and I enjoyed attending his lectures! The assessment and overall mark were satisfactory, although the exams were challenging. He is lacking in responsiveness to emails!

He 100% writes his own reviews. All the bad reviews are completely accurate. He doesn't teach anything. The averages are around 40%. He blames the class for the low averages. This class felt satire. Just don't do it!!!
Knowledgeable and clear in the subjects. Heavy emphasis on both final and midterm exams. MC questions are challenging!
He makes the topics easy to understand, I really enjoyed taking this class with him.

Overall, a somewhat nice and pretty funny guy. Midterm was bad, but final was a lot easier, mainly because out of 5, the first 3 written questions were copied word for word from the midterm, and the fourth question was word for word number for number copied from the last assignment. He should provide more practice problems to help students learn.

yall im being so deadass when i say avoid unless your amazing at teaching urself. everything we learn in class is using excel&tableau software but the exams are only handwritten and he doesn't teach u how to do anything by hand u learn on ur own. also doesn't provide any sample questions that reflect the only handwritten method all of them use exel
